title: phlegmariurus weberbaueri
intro: Phlegmariurus weberbaueri, also known as Weberbauer's climbing forked polypody, is a type of epiphytic fern in the family Lycopodiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. The health benefits of Phlegmariurus weberbaueri include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and its dried leaves can be used in infusions to take advantage of its health benefits.
